submitted_payload.agent_description: Free and open-source workflow layer for autonomous coding that composes planning, implementation, and verification so teams can review finished, tested code instead of another confident summary.

verification.execution_method: Direct POST to the public Django form on /submit/ using a fresh CSRF token and session cookie from the live page.
verification.success_signal: Submission Successful page rendered after POST.

why_this_action: The latest audit says to pause another broad low-intent directory burst and another same-family curator batch, so the best fresh move is a single higher-intent developer-facing surface that can be executed immediately.
AiAgents.Directory is adjacent to open-source developer tools and AI agent discovery, which is closer to Ralph Workflow's real evaluation path than another generic AI-tools directory.
The target was already discovered in shared artifacts, but it was not yet logged in outreach-log.md, so this run expands distribution instead of duplicating an in-flight lane.
The submission keeps Codeberg as the primary destination and GitHub as the mirror destination.
expected_outcome: A fresh developer-facing listing path that points qualified evaluators to the Codeberg repo first and can later become a measurable backlink or referral source.
replacement_condition: If there is no live AiAgents.Directory listing or referral evidence by 2026-06-07, treat this as pending-only and use the next fresh-action slot on a different executable high-intent lane instead of another same-family directory replay.
